page: Amazon Web Services g7.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The system features 96 dedicated physical cores on an Intel x86_64 architecture running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

For hardware acceleration, the instance bundles four NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 graphics processing units, delivering a combined 128 GB of video RAM. Local storage consists of 3800 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 350 Gbps.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g7.24xlarge offers a highly dense resource profile, combining multi-GPU capabilities with high-speed local NVMe storage and high network throughput. The dedicated core allocation ensures predictable compute performance, though the lack of hyperthreading means workloads must rely on raw physical core scaling.

This hardware configuration is designed for graphics-intensive workloads, multi-GPU rendering, machine learning inference, and high-throughput data processing tasks that require substantial local storage and high-speed network connectivity.
description: Amazon Web Services g7.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated physical Intel x86_64 cores operating at 3.9 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of system memory. The hardware configuration includes four bundled NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 GPUs with a total of 128 GB of VRAM, alongside 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 350 Gbps. This instance provides a high-density resource profile suitable for demanding multi-GPU workloads, rendering, and data-intensive processing. The dedicated core allocation ensures consistent compute performance without hyperthreading overhead. It is optimized for graphics rendering, machine learning inference, and high-throughput data processing workloads.

tagline: A high-density GPU instance featuring four dedicated graphics processors, high-speed NVMe storage, and 350 Gbps network bandwidth for intensive workloads.
